articles.title: Warning oil could surge to $150 a barrel with inventories 'critically low'

children.content: A major Australian bank has warned the price of oil is at risk of surging to $150 per barrel as oil inventories approach critically low levels. Follow live.

children.content: The US president says his military has been running a "secret mission" to help tankers and commercial ships defy Iran's blockade.

image.altText: Donald Trump sits in a leather chair.

articles.title: Sea drone saves US helicopter crew downed in waters near Strait of Hormuz

items.title: State disability ministers lash NDIS overhaul

children.content: State and territory disability ministers have accused the Albanese government of pushing on with NDIS reform that does not align with previous agreements.

items.title: Culture in Australia's largest police force 'unacceptable'

children.content: Bullying, discrimination and sexual harassment are occurring at "unacceptable levels" within the NSW Police Force and employees are too afraid to speak up, an independent report has found.

image.altText: Uniformed Police officers survey a busy protest

items.title: Melbourne aged care COVID outbreak back in spotlight

children.content: An inquest examining 50 COVID-era deaths at a Melbourne aged care home has resumed after a years-long hiatus.

image.altText: A sign.

items.title: Former bikie boss arrested in Bali hiding in toilet of private jet

children.content: Former bikie boss Angelo Pandeli was attempting to fly to Africa on a fake Brazilian passport when Indonesian immigration authorities apprehended him over an Interpol notice.

items.title: How anti-immigration figures used Belfast stabbing to push an agenda

children.content: Billionaire Elon Musk and far-right activist Tommy Robinson have seized upon the alleged stabbing of a man in Belfast by a Sudanese immigrant to push an anti-immigration agenda.

items.title: Grandfather's fall inspires 20yo student's smart clock invention

items.title: Consumer watchdog warns real estate agents must not misrepresent homes

children.content: Real estate agents selling homes must not misrepresent information about the builder or serious defects, SA's consumer watchdog has said, after eight builders were hit with public warning notices over their conduct in the state.

articles.title: Injury concern for Socceroos star on eve of World Cup

image.altText: Mo Toure looks sad during a game for the Socceroos.

articles.title: Mexico City welcomes World Cup but reality bites for many

children.content: As Mexicans prepare to host or co-host the FIFA World Cup for a record third time, many locals are frustrated by being locked out of the tournament, while others are more optimistic.

items.title: World's biggest whale graveyard found in Indian Ocean off Australia

children.content: Hundreds of whale fossils, including those from extinct species new to science, range in age from recent to 5 million years old and are also home to undescribed deep-sea creatures.

image.altText: Underwater vision of a whale fossil on the seafloor.

items.title: Casey McLean's Origin arrival is proof the future has come

items.title: Taylor rules out One Nation seat carve-up as party raises $2m

children.content: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says the Coalition has no plan to share the seats the party targets at the next federal election with One Nation as Pauline Hanson's party says it has raised $2 million since launch.

image.altText: An older man wearing a suit gives an impassioned speech.

items.title: Government flags risk of relying on Elon Musk's SpaceX and Starlink

image.altText: Rizwan Rana in a Ralph Lauren polo shirt standing in front of his Lexus.

items.title: 'It is what it is': Solomon Islands says it can't release details of China security deal

items.title: Beachside towns in WA face difficult decisions as erosion gets worse

children.content: A week after Western Australia experienced one of its worst storms in decades, some stretches of coast are crumbling into the ocean at an alarming rate. Millions of dollars' worth of infrastructure is at risk heading into winter.

items.title: $100k payout after theft of iconic Tasmanian wool trophy

children.content: The trophy features a sculpture that includes a 24-carat gold merino sheep and a weaving loom made of silver and platinum, with its theft last year captured on CCTV.

items.title: Predictive text in 'demonstrable decline' with AI-based language models

children.content: If internet chatter is to be believed, the intuitiveness and efficiency of predictive text and autocorrect have fallen off a cliff in recent years. But while researchers agree there has been a demonstrable decline in quality, the increasing sophistication of what phones can do is also making errors more prominent.

image.altText: a close up of a person holding a mobile phone and texting

items.title: Daylesford pub had no outdoor dining permit at time of deadly crash, court told

children.content: A Victorian court hears that a regional pub where five people died when a driver had a hypoglycaemic episode, crashing his car through diners, did not have a valid outdoor dining permit at the time of the crash.

image.altText: Two water jugs, paper cups and a bucket sit on a table that has a broken wooden stool.

items.title: 'People are just killing themselves': Solariums operating underground

children.content: Illegal solariums are operating across the country, from single machines secretly set up in suburban neighbourhood garages to those hidden in the back of legitimate businesses.

items.title: Outback airship push uncertain as talks 'stall'

children.content: Giant cargo airships based in outback Queensland are being pitched as a freight solution for remote Australia, but questions remain about how the project will go ahead.

items.title: Girlfriend of man shot by police disputes that officers told him to drop knife

items.title: Knicks pull off Madison Square Garden miracle in NBA Finals

children.content: The New York Knicks complete the greatest comeback in NBA Finals history, rallying from 29 points down to beat San Antonio in game four and move to the brink of a first title in 53 years.

image.altText: A big screen shows 'KNICKS WIN' as fans and players celebrate on court at the NBA Finals.

items.title: Fraudsters jailed for stealing millions from 'older and vulnerable' victims

children.content: Two men who "exploited the vulnerability" of multiple victims by swindling millions of dollars in a fraudulent property investment scheme have been jailed for the "undoubtedly serious" offending.

items.title: Pope Leo blesses Barcelona's Sagrada Familia as an 'architectural masterpiece'

children.content: The pontiff has celebrated Spain's Sagrada Familia Basilica as a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light," as he marked the centenary of the death of its architect Antoni Gaudí.

Pope Leo and Catholic Church clergy members in white and gold robes standing under large organs and a sculpture of Jesus.

items.title: What happens when a gas company abandons an entire city?
